Cinnamomum is a type of evergreen tree in the laurel family that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been associated with various health benefits, including digestive support and relief from respiratory issues.

The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum include cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, linalool, and eugenol, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties.

In traditional herbal medicine, Cinnamomum is often used to make various preparations, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils.
